mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in retail sales (RSAFS) indicates robust consumer demand, which translates to higher corporate revenues and earnings, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If the 20-day forward returns do not show a statistically significant positive correlation with RSAFS month-over-month growth over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A significant drop in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) below a critical threshold signals a tightening labor market, which can lead to wage inflation and reduced corporate profit margins, negatively impacting S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: If S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of instances where UNRATE drops below 4.5%.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation (CPIAUCSL) is rising and the Federal Funds Rate (FEDFUNDS) is also increasing, it indicates an aggressive monetary tightening cycle to combat inflation, which typically dampens equity valuations due to higher discount rates and potential recession fears, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3mo avg change > 0.5% AND FEDFUNDS 3mo avg change > 0.1%
failure_condition: If S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 40% of periods where both CPIAUCSL and FEDFUNDS are rising as specified.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of strong economic growth (high GDPC1), investor confidence is high, leading to increased risk appetite and positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days, as corporate earnings are expected to improve.
failure_condition: If the correlation between GDPC1 year-over-year growth and S&P 500 20-day forward returns is negative or statistically insignificant during identified economic expansion periods.

mechanism_narrative: A significant decline in industrial production (INDPRO) below a certain level indicates a weakening manufacturing sector and broader economic slowdown, which negatively impacts corporate earnings and S&P 500 forward returns over a 5-day horizon.
failure_condition: If S&P 500 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of instances where INDPRO shows a significant 3-month decline.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent job creation (PAYEMS growth) reflects a healthy labor market, supporting consumer spending and corporate profitability, thereby driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: If the 60-day forward returns do not show a statistically significant positive correlation with PAYEMS month-over-month growth over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When housing starts (HOUST) are declining while the Federal Funds Rate (FEDFUNDS) is rising, it signals a significant slowdown in interest-rate sensitive sectors and tighter financial conditions, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST 3mo avg change < -5% AND FEDFUNDS 3mo avg change > 0.1%
failure_condition: If S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 45% of periods where HOUST is declining and FEDFUNDS is rising as specified.

mechanism_narrative: In a low-interest-rate environment (low FEDFUNDS), equities become more attractive relative to bonds, leading to higher valuations and positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 5-day horizon as investors seek yield.
failure_condition: If the S&P 500 5-day forward returns are not significantly positive when FEDFUNDS is below 1.0% over a 2-year rolling window.
